summary: SPARK's Spawner (src/game/spawner.ts, class at line 128) is a host/solo-only Poisson spark emitter with 5 independent mulberry32 streams (spark rng + bombRng + potatoRng + rainbowRng + seagullRng); S79 P5 added SpawnerState with getState()/restoreState() that round-trip all 5 RNG words plus countdown counters, fully unit-tested in src/game/spawner.test.ts but wired into NOTHING — the runtime instance is a module-local const in main.ts seeded from SEED 0xc0ffee with 4 xor-derived hazard streams, and no save/load path constructs or re-seeds it. src/state/save.ts owns WorldSnapshot (schemaVersion: 1, never bumped; all post-S15 fields are additive-optional, emitted only when non-empty/defined so older payloads stay byte-identical) and NetSnapshot, which is derived from the FULL snapshot by a fixed 4-key destructure-omit (savedAt, rngSeed, nextPrimitiveId, nextBondId) — therefore any new field emitted by snapshot() automatically rides the wire into every NetSnapshot unless explicitly added to both the Omit type and the runtime destructure. Today snapshot()/restore() are test-only (5 test files) and netSnapshot()/applyNetSnapshot() are net-only (HostSync at NET_SNAPSHOT_HZ=10); there is no save/load UI, no localStorage world persistence (localStorage holds only audio settings and codex unlocks), and no dev hotkeys or __SPARK__ accessor for saving. snapshot(world: World) cannot reach the Spawner at all — World (src/state/worldTypes.ts) has no spawner field, so wiring requires a new data path.
key_facts: Spawner class: src/game/spawner.ts:128; constructor (lines 159-176) takes (cfg, rng, bombRng=null, potatoRng=null, rainbowRng=null, seagullRng=null) — the 5 streams; null hazard stream = that hazard disabled (countdown Infinity, spawner.ts:310,328,346,364)

SpawnerState interface: spawner.ts:109-121 — 11 fields: nextId, secondsUntilNextSpawn, sparksUntilBomb/Potato/Rainbow/Seagull (number|null; null encodes Infinity/disabled via fin() at spawner.ts:242), rngState (uint32 word), bombRngState/potatoRngState/rainbowRngState/seagullRngState (number|null)

getState(): spawner.ts:237-258 — returns null if ANY enabled stream is not a StatefulRng (isStateful guard spawner.ts:124-126); caller is expected to fall back to from-seed behaviour per the docblock (spawner.ts:233-236)

restoreState(s): spawner.ts:266-281 — nullish-coalesces null countdowns to Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY, calls setState(word) per enabled stream; docblock (260-265) requires the spawner be constructed with the SAME stream configuration the state was captured under

S79 P5 unit tests: src/game/spawner.test.ts:236-313 — 3 tests: (1) 267-293 getState→restoreState resumes ALL five sequences bit-exactly mid-stream (restores into a DIFFERENT-seed spawner, 60s warm + 60s compare, asserts every hazard actually fired), (2) 295-298 getState returns null for custom non-stateful Rng, (3) 300-312 disabled streams round-trip as null and stay idle
fullSpawner test helper (spawner.test.ts:242-250) mirrors main.ts construction exactly: mulberry32(seed), seed^0x9e3779b9, seed^0x85ebca6b, seed^0xc2b2ae35, seed^0x5a4e28b8

Runtime instance: src/main.ts:227-234 — module-local const inside main(); SEED=0xc0ffee (main.ts:210); rng=mulberry32(SEED) (214); bombRng=SEED^0x9e3779b9 (217); potatoRng=SEED^0x85ebca6b (220); rainbowRng=SEED^0xc2b2ae35 (223); seagullRng=SEED^0x5a4e28b8 (226)

Spawner ticks only in stepPhysics (src/physics/physicsLoop.ts:66), called from main.ts:553 gated on world.gameState==='PLAYING' && !isClient (main.ts:551-552) — spawner NEVER runs on the 1v1 client; hazard caps (BOMB/POTATO/RAINBOW/SEAGULL_MAX_ACTIVE) enforced at dispatch site (physicsLoop.ts:70-95) with skip-and-redraw

NO restore path touches the spawner: main.ts never imports snapshot/restore (grep: zero matches); restore() writes world.rngSeed/nextPrimitiveId/nextBondId only (save.ts:499-501); the Spawner keeps its boot-time stream words forever — a restore today does NOT re-seed it (acknowledged latent in spawner.ts:102-108 docblock)

World has NO spawner field: src/state/worldTypes.ts:37-120 — snapshot(world: World) (save.ts:420) cannot reach the Spawner; wiring requires a new data path (spawner state onto World, or a changed snapshot signature)

WorldSnapshot: save.ts:71-166 — required: schemaVersion:1 (literal type, line 72), savedAt, tick, rngSeed, gameState, lastWinnerId, nextPrimitiveId, nextBondId, freeSparks, primitives, bonds, players; optional: scoreProgress, gameMode, currentPlayerId (ignored since S42), scoreByPlayer, creatures, bombs, hunters, hunterSpawned, potatoes, rainbows, seagulls, poops, fouledPrimitives, effects

Versioning: schemaVersion locked at 1 since S15; restore() throws on mismatch (save.ts:493-495), applyNetSnapshot() too (542-544); every addition since S15 used the additive-optional pattern with NO version bump (precedent chain cited at save.ts:287-288: S15 P2 / S28 P0 / S31 P0-3 / S33 P1-11 / S36 P3)

Emit-only-when-present discipline: snapshot() emits optional collections only when non-empty (creatures save.ts:444-446, bombs 449-451, hunters 454-456, hunterSpawned 458, potatoes 460-462, rainbows 464-466, seagulls/poops/fouledPrimitives 468-472, effects 480-488) so undefined keys are dropped by JSON.stringify and older payloads stay byte-identical

S81 precedent for additive-optional fields: SerializedPotato.carriedAtTick (save.ts:380-383) — emitted only while CARRIED via spread-conditional in serializePotato (save.ts:1084), rehydrated as possibly-undefined (save.ts:1099); same conditional-spread pattern in serializePlayer for territorialShrinkUntilTick/benchedUntilTick/carriedPotatoId (save.ts:834-845)

CRITICAL — NetSnapshot derivation: NetSnapshot = Omit<WorldSnapshot,'savedAt'|'rngSeed'|'nextPrimitiveId'|'nextBondId'> (save.ts:514-517); netSnapshot() calls snapshot(world) then destructure-omits exactly those 4 literal keys (save.ts:523-534) — ANY new field added to snapshot() output leaks into NetSnapshot (and the wire) unless added to BOTH the Omit type and the runtime destructure

rngSeed is DELIBERATELY excluded from NetSnapshot ('deterministic RNG not v1', save.ts:19-21); a spawner-state field is the same class of host-only data and would be present on essentially every host snapshot during PLAYING, so unlike the rare-hazard optionals it would change wire bytes on EVERY NetSnapshot if not omitted
Wire-omission test: src/net/sync.test.ts:42-47 asserts savedAt/rngSeed/nextPrimitiveId/nextBondId are undefined on msg.snapshot — it enumerates the 4 known keys and would NOT catch a NEW leaked field

Wire path: HostSync.buildSnapshotMessage (src/net/sync.ts:29-36) wraps netSnapshot(world) in a NETSNAPSHOT envelope at NET_SNAPSHOT_HZ=10 (sync.ts:4); applyNetSnapshot applies via shared applySnapshotCore (save.ts:541-546) which never writes host-only fields

Replay test: src/state/save.replay.test.ts — determinismJson() strips savedAt then JSON.stringify's snapshot() (lines 36-41); asserts byte-equal JSON across two same-seed runs of a 1000-iter and 250-iter reducer stress script (94-123), different-seed divergence canary via the rngSeed field (125-141), plus S34 PB-5 creature-lifecycle determinism (197-241); spawner is NOT exercised (runStress hand-mints sparks, never ticks a Spawner)

save.test.ts (src/state/save.test.ts:43-71+) asserts snapshot→JSON→restore round-trips: primitives/bonds/tick/rngSeed/gameState equality, bond adjacency + a/b ref reconstruction, stiffness tiers; later sections cover bombs/hunters/potatoes (imports applySpawnBomb/applySpawnHunter/applySpawnPotato, lines 12-14)

snapshot/restore consumers are TEST-ONLY: src/game/session15.test.ts:29, src/state/save.test.ts:11, src/state/world.test.ts:15, src/state/seagulls/seagull.test.ts:31, src/state/save.replay.test.ts:26; netSnapshot/applyNetSnapshot consumers: src/net/sync.ts:20 (production), sync.test.ts:16, creatureLifecycle.test.ts:46

NO save/load UI exists: localStorage in src is audio settings only (src/render/audioManager.ts:177-199,616-620) and codex unlocks (src/render/codexOverlay.ts:32-44); main.ts keydown handlers are only R (postgame reset), C (cinematics toggle), M (mute) at main.ts:472-488; __SPARK__ DEV accessor (main.ts:437-449) exposes world/controls/netTransport/lobbyScreen/titleScreen/fogRenderer/potatoRenderer/aboveFogLayer/app — no spawner, no save hooks; save.ts comments referencing 'localStorage saves' (lines 90, 257) are historical, no such path exists in code

StatefulRng: src/state/rng.ts:15-18 — interface extends Rng with getState():number / setState(state:number):void; mulberry32 (rng.ts:20-33) implements it: getState returns the uint32 word t, setState sets t=state>>>0; whole generator state is one uint32

No dedicated rng.test.ts exists (Glob: only src/state/rng.ts); StatefulRng coverage lives in spawner.test.ts S79 block; byte-drift locking tests: spawner.test.ts:63-78 (same seed → identical sequence), 142-160 (adding bombRng leaves spark stream byte-identical), 188-214 (adding rainbowRng leaves spark+bomb+potato byte-identical), plus save.replay.test.ts byte-equal JSON; pseudoRand byte-exactness guarded by save.replay.test.ts per rng.ts:59 comment

Stream-derivation precedent also used per-tick elsewhere: rainbowLifecycle.ts:105 builds a one-shot mulberry32((world.rngSeed ^ Math.imul(world.tick, 2654435761))>>>0) — stateless, replay-safe, not part of the Spawner's 5 streams
PHYSICS_HZ=60 (src/constants.ts:151); SPAWN_RATE_PER_SECOND=0.15 default with test override hook (constants.ts:91)

key_files.role: Spawner class, SpawnerState interface, getState/restoreState (S79 P5), 5-stream RNG design, enforceSpawnerBounds
Spawner unit tests incl. S79 P5 state round-trip block (lines 236-313) and byte-drift stream-separation tests
WorldSnapshot + NetSnapshot definitions, snapshot/restore/netSnapshot/applyNetSnapshot, the 4-key destructure-omit (514-534), all serialize/deserialize helpers
Rng type, StatefulRng interface (15-18), mulberry32 with getState/setState, pseudoRand one-shot hash
Runtime Spawner construction + seeding (210-234), physics tick gate (550-574), __SPARK__ DEV accessor (437-449), keydown handlers (472-488); never imports snapshot/restore
stepPhysics — sole production spawner.tick() call site (line 66) + hazard-cap dispatch (70-95)
Replay-determinism guard: byte-equal snapshot JSON (savedAt stripped) across same-seed runs; reducer + creature stress; does NOT exercise the Spawner
WorldSnapshot round-trip unit tests (chain, stiffness tiers, bombs/hunters/potatoes rehydration)
HostSync.buildSnapshotMessage → netSnapshot(world) at NET_SNAPSHOT_HZ=10; ClientSync applyNetSnapshot + lerp
Asserts the 4 host-only fields are absent from the wire snapshot (lines 42-47) — enumerated, won't catch new leaks
World interface — confirms NO spawner field exists on World (snapshot() cannot reach the Spawner today)

constraints: NetSnapshot wire bytes are locked: netSnapshot() derives from the FULL snapshot() by removing exactly 4 literal keys (save.ts:514-534); any new WorldSnapshot field emitted by snapshot() leaks onto the wire on EVERY host snapshot unless added to BOTH the Omit type union AND the runtime destructure
schemaVersion must stay 1 — every addition since S15 is additive-optional with no version bump; restore()/applyNetSnapshot() throw on any other value (save.ts:493-495, 542-544)

Additive-optional fields must be emitted only when present/defined (conditional spread or non-empty guard) so JSON.stringify drops undefined keys and pre-existing saves/payloads stay byte-identical — S81 P2 carriedAtTick (save.ts:380-383, 1084) is the latest precedent

RNG streams are locked: main.ts SEED=0xc0ffee and the 4 xor constants (0x9e3779b9, 0x85ebca6b, 0xc2b2ae35, 0x5a4e28b8) define the production stream configuration; restoreState requires the SAME stream configuration the state was captured under (spawner.ts:260-265)
Spawner is host/solo-only (stepPhysics gated on !isClient, main.ts:551-553); the 1v1 client must never construct/tick/restore a spawner-driven sim

save.replay.test.ts asserts byte-equal JSON.stringify of snapshot() minus savedAt across same-seed runs — any new snapshot field must be deterministic under that harness (note: its runStress never ticks a Spawner, so spawner state is currently invisible to it)
sync.test.ts:42-47 asserts savedAt/rngSeed/nextPrimitiveId/nextBondId are undefined on the wire snapshot — this contract must keep holding; it does not protect against NEW leaked fields
getState() can legitimately return null (non-stateful custom Rng) — any persistence wiring must handle the null fallback (from-seed behaviour) per spawner.ts:233-236

snapshot(world: World) signature: World has no spawner field (worldTypes.ts:37-120) — the Spawner is unreachable from the existing serializeWorld entry point; this is a structural gap, not an oversight
Deterministic fixed-tick sim at PHYSICS_HZ=60 (constants.ts:151); spawner.tick consumes PHYSICS_DT=1/60 (physicsLoop.ts:47,66)
risks: Wire-leak risk is concrete, not theoretical: unlike rare-hazard optionals (bombs/seagulls emitted only when non-empty), a spawnerState field would be non-undefined on essentially every host snapshot during PLAYING, changing bytes of every 10Hz NetSnapshot unless added to the omission list in save.ts:514-534
sync.test.ts's absence-assertions are an enumerated allowlist (4 fields) — a new leaked field passes CI silently; there is no generic 'NetSnapshot contains only retained keys' guard

restoreState silently skips streams when configuration mismatches (put() guards null stream / null word, spawner.ts:274-280) — restoring a 5-stream state into a spawner built with fewer streams, or vice versa, degrades without error; only the docblock contract (spawner.ts:262-264) prevents misuse

restore() already has a latent semantics gap the S79 docblock names (spawner.ts:102-108): world.rngSeed round-trips but nothing reconstructs RNG word state from it — main.ts's spawner keeps its boot-time words after any (test-only) restore, so save/load cannot resume the spawn sequence today

Spawner.nextId mints SparkIds (spawner.ts:284); after a hypothetical load without restoreState, a fresh spawner restarts nextId=0 and would mint SparkIds colliding with rehydrated freeSparks (the bomb/hunter/potato maps got max-id+1 counter advancement in applySnapshotCore, save.ts:589-665, but SparkId minting lives in the Spawner, not World)

save.replay.test.ts would NOT currently detect spawner-state non-determinism: its stress scripts dispatch hand-built sparks and never tick a Spawner, so wiring spawner state into snapshot() adds a field the existing replay harness only covers trivially (constant across runs only if capture points align)

Comments in save.ts (lines 90, 257) reference 'existing localStorage saves (S15-S41)' — historical user data may exist in browsers from earlier sessions even though no current code path reads or writes world saves; any strict-parse change could be checked against that legacy shape
open_questions: Where is SpawnerState intended to live in the persisted shape — a new optional WorldSnapshot field (which then MUST be added to the NetSnapshot Omit + destructure) was the S79 intent per spawner.ts:102-108, but snapshot(world) cannot reach the Spawner; the goal-context wiring needs a data path decision (spawner-state-on-World vs. changed serialize signature) that is out of scope for this map

Should the existing world.rngSeed field interact with restored spawner words (e.g., as fallback when getState() returned null), given rngSeed currently round-trips but is consumed only by rainbowLifecycle's per-tick one-shot derivation (rainbowLifecycle.ts:105)?

Is there an MCV verification[] binding (mentioned in S81 close commit 32ca21f) that asserts on snapshot/wire shape and would need updating alongside any WorldSnapshot addition? Not found under src/ during this pass

Spawner.nextId vs. world's SparkId space: freeSparks rehydrate with their original ids, but no max-spark-id+1 advancement exists anywhere (unlike bombs/hunters/potatoes/rainbows/seagulls/poops in applySnapshotCore) — confirm whether SpawnerState.nextId restoration is the intended sole defense against SparkId collision after load
